demographic_audit_note: Verified Medicare Advantage Plan; identified and corrected a typo in the primary contact phone number in Atlantis by cross-referencing the facility face sheet. Confirmed home address is within the MA's 30-mile travel radius.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Home. SW confirmed the patient has a strong support system and the goal is a return to her ranch-style home with skilled nursing for diabetic wound management.
stage_1_intake_responses: Q1: Yes, patient's neighbor used the service. Q2: Thinks we provide the actual wound care. Q3: SW mentioned nursing but not the MA transition visit. Q4: Expressed high anxiety about the frequency of dressing changes; PN escalated this specific concern to the SW to ensure the HHA frequency is clearly defined.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: Wound showing granular tissue; patient cleared by PT for home discharge. SW targets a Friday discharge. PN confirmed with HHA that the referral was received and logged in the Hub.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card inserted into patient's smartphone and daughter's phone; program flyer delivered and reviewed at bedside.
hha_confirmation_status: Initially 'Not In Place'. HHA intake reported the assigned wound care specialist is out with a sudden illness. PN investigated and found no backup specialist available for the Friday SOC. Following escalation, SW and HHA agreed to a generalist nurse for the Friday bridge visit with the specialist returning Monday. HHA is now 'In Place' (SOC Friday, Nurse: Sarah R., Window: 12-2 PM).
stage_3_followup_audit: Call 1 (SW): Confirmed D/C date is firm for Friday morning. Call 2 (Daughter): Confirmed she understands the HHA nurse arrives first for the SOC and the MA will follow within 24 hours to reinforce the care plan.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached patient's daughter 24 hours prior to discharge; confirmed the hospital bed and wound care supplies were delivered to the home. Daughter feels 'confident' with the Day 1 schedule.
atlantis_final_sync: Entered D/C date (Friday) and 1st MA visit date (Saturday) into Atlantis. Physical appointment card handed to patient during final bedside visit.
ma_visit_booking: MA visit successfully scheduled for Saturday morning. Booking was held until Thursday afternoon when the HHA finally confirmed the bridge nurse assignment, satisfying the HHA-First Rule.
narrative_summary: Patient Maria, a 69-year-old with a diabetic foot ulcer, was successfully transitioned home despite a critical staffing gap. During the Confidence Audit, I identified that the HHA's primary specialist was ill, creating a 'Not In Place' status. By categorizing this as a 'Provider Illness' barrier and escalating to the SW, a bridge nurse was secured, allowing me to safely schedule the MA within the 24-hour window without risking a wasted visit. The Connection Intake early in the stay allowed us to address Maria's clinical anxiety through SW intervention, ensuring a stable handoff.

format_1_state_log.event_description: HHA reports assigned wound nurse is out sick; no immediate backup specialist identified.
PN escalates staffing gap to SW; HHA intake confirms a generalist nurse will bridge the Friday/Sunday dressing changes.

format_2_triples.situation: Connection Intake Q4 surfaces patient fear of wound infection at home.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Escalated patient anxiety to the Social Worker.

format_2_triples.situation: HHA coordinator is vague about the start-of-care time window.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Performed Deep_Dive_HHA_Barrier call to identify specific staffing illness.

format_2_triples.situation: HHA confirms bridge nurse is assigned and window is set.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Scheduled the MA for the 24-hour post-discharge visit.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Upon hearing the HHA nurse is sick, the PN waits for the Social Worker to receive the automated agency update and contact the family regarding the potential discharge delay.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the SW's role as the primary coordinator of the discharge timeline but fails to proactively resolve the 'In Place' status required for MA scheduling.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: PN calls the HHA intake coordinator to confirm the specific barrier (Provider Illness), then informs the SW that the MA cannot be scheduled until a bridge nurse is assigned, providing the SW with the specific info needed to pressure the agency.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This follows the 'HHA-First Rule' and the 'Deep_Dive_HHA_Barrier' protocol, ensuring the MA is only scheduled when the transition is verified.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: PN contacts a different HHA agency known to have wound specialists available and suggests to the SW that the referral be pulled and redirected to this new agency to avoid a discharge delay.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This violates the SW Boundary and the Banned Actions list by suggesting specific HHA agencies and interfering with the SW's referral management.
